Gene_Accession_Number.reason: The paper identifies PsFAR as gene id PSOL02039 in the Results and reports the full-length sequence with GenBank accession number OM371326.

Source_Species.reason: PsFAR was studied in the cotton mealybug throughout the paper.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The studied species is explicitly Phenacoccus solenopsis.

Applied_Species.reason: Functional assays were performed on the cotton mealybug.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: RNAi, CHC, SEM, desiccation, and insecticide assays were all done in Phenacoccus solenopsis.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Results show PsFAR knockdown reduced cuticular hydrocarbons (CHCs), and SEM showed reduced wax output/waxy layer.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: CHCs are explicitly hydrocarbons and are discussed as major cuticular lipids/wax components.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: The paper repeatedly describes wax/waxy filaments on the body surface/epicuticular surface and integument, and CHCs were measured from mealybug wax.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Results show PsFAR knockdown increases mortality under desiccation and deltamethrin treatment, supporting roles in reducing water loss and protection against insecticide exposure.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: PsFAR is explicitly linked to cuticular hydrocarbon (CHC) biosynthesis and wax biosynthesis in the Results.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: The Introduction describes FAR-catalyzed conversion to alcohols as a step in CHC biosynthesis, and PsFAR is the FAR implicated in that pathway.

Enzyme_Name.reason: PsFAR is explicitly described as a fatty acyl-CoA reductase gene.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Sequence analysis states PsFAR has an N-terminal Rossmann fold NAD(P)+-binding domain, sterile alpha motif protein domain, cofactor-binding motif TGXXGF, and active-site motif YXXXK.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The paper states FAR catalyzes the conversion to alcohols in the CHC pathway and cites Ericerus pela FAR converting acyl-CoA to the corresponding alcohol; PsFAR is treated as the FAR in this pathway.

Primary_Substrate.reason: The pathway description attributes FAR function to fatty acyl-CoA substrates in CHC biosynthesis.

Primary_Product.reason: The paper states the FAR step is conversion to alcohols in the CHC pathway.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: PsFAR acts before CHC end products, so 'midstream' is plausible, but the paper does not explicitly label the step as midstream.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: Results directly connect PsFAR knockdown to reduced CHCs and reduced wax output, supporting an intermediate/precursor supply role for end-product formation.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper supports PsFAR as crucial/important for CHC and wax production, but does not establish formal rate-limiting kinetics; this field appropriately includes that caveat.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Figure 2c and text state expression is highest in third-instar nymphs and adult females, lowest in adult males, and significantly highest in fat body of adult females.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: This matches the measured expression profile in Phenacoccus solenopsis.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: All listed factors appear in the Results: dsRNA microinjection, transgenic tobacco-mediated RNAi, low humidity (<10% RH), and deltamethrin treatment.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: These methods are all reported and used for PsFAR identification/validation: transcriptomics, proteomics, RACE, RT-qPCR, RNAi by injection and transgenic tobacco, GC-MS, SEM, desiccation assay, and insecticide assay.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show knockdown reduces CHC contents and wax layer, decreases desiccation survival under <10% RH, and increases deltamethrin susceptibility.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each listed point matches the Results: PSOL02039 identified by omics, expression highest in fat body/third instar/adult female, RNAi reduced transcript and CHCs/wax, and knockdown increased desiccation and insecticide mortality.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper demonstrates host-induced RNAi via transgenic tobacco and discusses destroying the wax layer as a management strategy, supporting pest-control target value.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The paper reports mortality and unsuccessful molting after PsFAR knockdown; it does not assess crop breeding value, so this statement is consistent with the paper.

Omics_Data.reason: Data availability explicitly lists RNA-seq accession PRJNA798788 and proteome accession PASS01729.

Other_Important_Info.reason: TEM showed no obvious envelope integrity change, discussion states PsFAR mainly affects waxy filaments rather than envelope, CHC classes/types remained while several n-alkanes decreased, and other FAR paralogs were not functionally validated as core genes.
